II. A alone takes 16 hours to fill the tank.
III. B alone takes 24 hours to fill the tank.

Q9 Not checked
General Aptitude • CIVIL ENGINEERING • Intermediate • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Pointing to a photograph of a boy Rashid said, “He is the son of the only son of my mother. “How is Rashid related to that boy?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The boy in the photograph is the only son of the son of Rashid’s mother. i.e. the son of Rashid.
Hence, Rashid is the father.

Q10 Not checked
General Aptitude • CIVIL ENGINEERING • Intermediate • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A cube is painted red on all faces and it is cut into 125 smaller cubes of equal size.

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c, d)
1. Number of cubes which have three faces painted = 8 (because 8 corners)
2. Number of cubes wihich have two faces painted = (5 – 2) × No. of edges = (5 – 2) × 12 = 36
3. Number of cubes which have one face painted = (5 – 2)2 × 6 = 54
4. Number of cubes with no faces painted = (5 – 2)3 × 6 = 27 (Interior cubes)

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Phase margin can be calculated for any system whereas gain margin cannot be calculated for all the systems.

Solution

(a)
A3H is for single side band with full carrier. Therefore, match (1) is incorrect.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)
• Since an n-type semiconductor has a much larger concentration of electrons than holes, it is easy to exceed the equilibrium hole concentration by creating excess carriers. The same is true for electrons in a p-type semiconductor. Hence, relative change in minority carrier concentration is more compare to majority carrier concentration in non equilibrium state with excess carriers.
• In equilibrium, we have a constant Fermi level. In non-equilibrium with excess carriers, Fermi levels are generalized to separate quasi-Fermi levels for electrons and holes. The quasi-Fermi level splitting is a measure of the departure from equilibrium.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is not correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
KVL assumes that magnetic fields don’t change in a closed circuit. It can’t be applied when there are fluctuating magnetic field. A changing magnetic field would act as another source of emf in the circuit.

Q55 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about controller and compensator:
1. A controller is used to manipulate a variable in a process, while a compensator is used to regulate another system within a control system.
2. A lag compensator adds negative phase to a system, while a lead compensator adds positive phase.
3. A lead compensator can increase the stability or speed of response of a system.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A controller manipulates a variable in a process whereas compensator regulates another system by conditioning the input or output to that system.
• A lag compensator adds negative phase to the system over the specified frequency range, while a lead compensator adds positive phase over the specified frequency range.
• A phase-lead compensator tends to shift the root locus toward the left half plane. This results in an improvement in the system's stability and an increase in the response speed.
Hence, all the statements are correct.
